hypothesis: Incorporating ternary metal combinations (two distinct metals sharing the same chemical template lattice) into the ML-guided chemical template workflow will yield stable ternary superhydrides with non-integer hydrogen-to-metal ratios exhibiting superconducting transition temperatures exceeding those of their binary analogues, because the electronic heterogeneity introduced by mixed metal sites will enhance electron-phonon coupling through additional low-frequency phonon modes while preserving the three-dimensional hydrogen clathrate network required for high Tc.
